{{Infobox sacred-art
| name = Hanging Tree
| introduced = The Crimson Vault
}}

''See also: [[Hanging Tree Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Hanging [[Trees]] are ancient, powerful entities used to maintain order and prevent total anarchy. Originally planted by ancestors, ten such trees exist, with each one guarded by an [[Overlord]]. These trees function by sealing Ragnarus [[Incarnations]] beneath them, a process that requires blood sacrifices. This method of containment is described as an agonizing experience for those involved, particularly for [[Incarnations]], who may remain conscious while being consumed. The trees must remain in contact with the earth and are capable of producing Ragnarus [[Gates]]. Beyond their role as prisons, the trees possess a sentient and violent nature; they can emit silent screams that cause intense mental agony and physical tremors in the surrounding area. When a tree dies, such as the one located in the [[Latari Forest]] or the one once standing in [[Cana]], its remains may transform into other forms, like wicker furniture. The trees can also be grown from seeds, which possess a potent and pulsing energy that can be used as a weapon. While they serve as a vital defense for the land, their use involves significant moral burdens due to the required sacrifices.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Hanging [[Tree]] is a blood-red Ragnarus sapling with crimson leaves that writhes with hunger and serves as a site for sacrifices. As an artifact of Ragnarus, these trees must remain in contact with the earth to seal away [[Incarnations]], meaning their destruction would allow such entities to escape. While the first nine trees were planted by ancestors and handed down through generations, [[King]] [[Zakareth]] planted a tenth tree and intends to defend it. Although [[Grandmaster Naraka]] attempted to incinerate the tree and [[Alin]]'s mission targeted its destruction, the [[Grandmasters]] failed to destroy even one. The tree remains a target for strikes in [[Cana]], though [[Tamara]] has since carried a seed to the Hanging [[Tree]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">18</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is a blood-red sapling that writhes with hunger and has crimson leaves.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is a force that [[Naraka]] was in the forest to sabotage.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is used for sacrifices.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is a blood-red [[Ragnarus tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Losing the [[Hanging Tree]] will release other [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Hanging Tree]] is an artifact of Ragnarus that seals [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Destroying more Hanging [[Trees]] allows [[Incarnations]] to escape.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] must be in contact with the earth.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Naraka]] attempts to summon power to incinerate the [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Grandmasters]] failed to destroy even one [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] was an object or location [[Alin]]'s mission intended to destroy.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] intends to defend the [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is a target for striking.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is a target for a strike team in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The first nine Hanging [[Trees]] were planted by the [[King]]'s ancestors.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] planted a tenth [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tamara]] carries a seed to the [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Hanging [[Trees]] have been handed down for generations.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Once located in the dungeon beneath the palace, the remnants of [[Cana]]'s Hanging [[Tree]] were eventually discovered at the bottom of a floor hole. These trees serve as a system for sealing [[Incarnations]], though they were never intended to function against [[Queen]] [[Cynara]]'s kind. While an ancient [[Incarnation]] remained sealed beneath the tree for more than three centuries, the process of sealing requires a cost of blood and can result in the trees failing individuals like [[Simon]]. Beyond their sealing capabilities, Hanging [[Trees]] possess the ability to feed on people to drain their power, and they can use crimson branches to swarm and devour targets.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The remnants of [[Cana]]'s [[Hanging Tree]] were found at the bottom of a hole in the floor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] once had a [[Hanging Tree]] in the dungeon beneath the palace.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Hanging [[Trees]] failed [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Incarnations]] were sealed underneath the Hanging [[Trees]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An ancient [[Incarnation]] was sealed beneath the [[Hanging Tree]] for over three centuries.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Hanging [[Trees]] can seal [[Incarnations]] at the cost of blood.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Hanging [[Trees]] are used for the system of sealing [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Hanging [[Trees]] can feed on a person and drain their power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] wondered if he was sealed underneath a [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] thought about who could be sealed beneath [[Cana]]'s [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Hanging [[Trees]] were not meant to work on [[Queen]] [[Cynara]]'s kind.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Hanging Tree]] can swarm and devour targets with crimson branches.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"One result of that bargain was the Hanging Trees, which can seal Incarnations at the cost of blood."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Sacred Arts]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
